body.title: Slate files: JSON format with prose_render field
body.result: JSON slates with backward-compat markdown read; create-slate, midstream, close, _append_to_todays_slate, stale_slate_check all updated
body.intention: I intend that slate files are JSON so agents can parse them without regex, with prose_render carrying the human-readable markdown render when it already exists.
body.description:
Slate files are currently freeform markdown. Convert to JSON with fields: date, in_flight[], planned[], done[], notes[], prose_render (optional — carried forward when it already exists, not generated). Slate writer (python run create-slate, midstream, close) emits JSON. Slate reader in context-load parses fields directly.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/slate.py (or equivalent writer), context-load skill, savestate skill
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; flat-file format (no Postgres dependency — slate must load before DB)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: slate file format + read/write tooling. Out of scope: DB-backed slates (already separate), bus envelopes, inference responses.
**Completion criteria:** T-slate-json-format is CLOSED; today's slate file is valid JSON; context-load parses in_flight/planned/done fields without regex; existing prose_render field preserved on read.
